preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: growth-focused SaaS teams who need a semantic design layer that adapts to visitor intent
brief.goal: demonstrate MODE's ability to generate intent-aware pages at funnel scale

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Hero (slim) provides page title and minimal context, orienting the growth-focused SaaS team on what they're about to commit to. Required on every page including conversion stage.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Conversion funnel stage demands a stripped sequence — Decision immediately follows Orientation to present pricing and plan comparison, framing the final choice without delay.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: CTA and commitment prompt placed immediately before Recovery per rules. At conversion stage this is a direct action capture — the visitor has already been through awareness and consideration elsewhere.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always-last beat providing secondary paths, footer links, and exit handling for visitors who aren't ready to convert despite reaching this stage.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Hero (slim) provides page title and minimal context, orienting the growth-focused SaaS team on what they're about to commit to. Required on every page including conversion stage.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Conversion funnel stage demands a stripped sequence — Decision immediately follows Orientation to present pricing and plan comparison, framing the final choice without delay.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: CTA and commitment prompt placed immediately before Recovery per rules. At conversion stage this is a direct action capture — the visitor has already been through awareness and consideration elsewhere.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Conversion stage demands focus on the primary decision moment below. The notes specify: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' This keeps the persistent CTA (most converted element on SaaS pages) without navigation noise.

Conversion funnel stage requires the text-only variant per the selection rules and component notes: 'text-only for conversion: slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.' Validator archetype with conversion goal demands clarity without distraction.

slots.headline: The intelligence layer your design system is missing.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register to the right experience for every archetype and funnel stage — without manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires trust-building specificity. The notes state: 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list.' The highlighted variant surfaces the recommended plan, and the extended description supports the Validator's need for comprehensive information before commitment.

slots.description: The full semantic intelligence layer: resolution engine, component manifest, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. Lives in your codebase, not a vendor dashboard.
features: Semantic resolution engine — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode
IA planner + multi-variant generation (Mover, Validator, Explorer)
Palette map with three out-of-box presets
Next.js preview UI with palette and label overlays
Lifetime access — no subscription, no lock-in

page.reasoning: Validator archetype at conversion stage requires trust signals. The notes specify: 'Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required').' Centered variant with subhead and trust_signal directly addresses validator skepticism at the decision moment.

slots.headline: One purchase. Full ownership. Running in under an hour.
slots.subhead: Configure your preset once, wire your brand tokens, and the semantic layer is operational before your first brief. Internal data.
slots.trust_signal: No subscription. No runtime dependency. Fork and extend freely.

page.reasoning: Conversion page requires recovery of departing users. The notes state: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.' This ensures a final conversion opportunity before page exit.
